global_cues.us_markets: Strong risk-on rally May 6 on US-Iran one-page MoU report: Nasdaq +2% to 25,326+; S&P 500 closed at fresh record (~7,300 area, first close above 7,300); Russell 2000 also at record. Dow mixed-to-higher (Nasdaq/S&P leadership, AI/tech bid). Oil collapsed from $114 to ~$100 (-9%) intraday on de-escalation; SoftBank ADR moves spilled into Asia.
global_cues.european_markets: European indices ripped higher May 6 on oil plunge / Iran deal hopes: DAX +2.12% to 24,918.69; FTSE 100 +2.15% to 10,438.66; CAC 40 +2.94% to 8,299.42; Stoxx 50 also +3% range. Inflation-relief bid across cyclicals/financials.
global_cues.asian_markets: Strongly positive on May 7 morning: Nikkei 225 +5% led by basic materials/tech/financials (Softbank +13%) as Japan returns from Golden Week with catch-up move; Hang Seng +1.47%; Kospi reversed early gains -0.68%. GIFT Nifty trading ~24,521 vs Nifty cash close 24,330.95 — signals firm gap-up open of ~150-180 pts.

fii_dii_summary: FIIs net sellers ₹5,834.90 Cr on May 6 — selling persisted despite the broader rally; DIIs net buyers ₹6,836.87 Cr, fully absorbing the foreign outflow for the first time this week. YTD FPI outflow ~₹1.92 lakh Cr (already exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh Cr); DII inflows ~₹1.7 lakh Cr YTD on sustained SIP support. DII firepower remains the structural cushion.

LUPIN (Lupin) - May 7 (Q4 FY26; recently received USFDA approval for ~$337M urea cycle drug — earnings call May 8)
BIOCON (Biocon) - May 7 (Q4 FY26 board meet; biosimilar pipeline focus)
PIDILITIND (Pidilite Industries) - May 7 (Q4 FY26; adhesives/construction chem demand read)
THERMAX (Thermax) - May 7 (Q4 FY26; capex/order-book bellwether)
ESCORTS (Escorts Kubota) - May 7 (Q4 FY26; tractors/farm equipment)
BHARATFORG (Bharat Forge) - May 7 (Q4 FY26 + final dividend; auto/defense forging)
MRF (MRF) - May 7 (Q4 FY26; tyres/raw-material pricing read)

BAJAJHLDNG (Bajaj Holdings & Investment) - May 7 (Q4 FY26)
IGL (Indraprastha Gas) - May 7 (Q4 FY26; CGD margins amid easing crude)
KARURVYSYA (Karur Vysya Bank) - May 7 (Q4 FY26)
COROMANDEL (Coromandel International) - May 7 (Q4 FY26; fertilizer/agri input)

GRAVITA (Gravita India) - May 7 (Q4 FY26; lead/aluminium recycling)

INDIGO is not reporting today; ~70 NSE-listed companies in total report May 7

ABB (ABB India) - May 8 (Q4 CY26 quarter; capex bellwether)
BANKBARODA (Bank of Baroda) - May 8 (Q4 FY26)
TITAN (Titan Company) - May 8 (Q4 FY26; jewellery demand + gold price impact)

macro_events: US-Iran MoU on Strait of Hormuz: one-page memo to halt West Asia conflict + nuclear-talks framework reportedly being signed; Trump paused 'Project Freedom' tanker escort — drove Brent from $114 down ~9% intraday to ~$100 and triggered global risk-on rally

RBI Deputy Governor Poonam Gupta (May 5) flagged India may consider lower inflation target / narrower tolerance band if growth-inflation mix stabilizes — inflation framework currently 4% +/- 2% (Apr-2026 to Mar-2031)
India CPI March 2026 print 3.4% YoY (12-month high but below 4% target); food inflation 3.87% — incremental easing helped by oil pullback today

Heavy Q4 FY26 earnings cluster May 6-9: ~70 companies report May 7 (DABUR, BRITANNIA, LUPIN, BIOCON, BHARATFORG, MRF, PIDILITIND, THERMAX, BSE, BAJAJHLDNG, IGL); ~76 on May 8 led by SBIN, TITAN, ABB, ASIANPAINT

April 2026 auto wholesales (released earlier this month) showed strong industry momentum — MARUTI record 1,87,704 units (+35% YoY); TATAMOTORS PV +30.5% YoY (~59k); M&M domestic +8% YoY (56,331); industry total ~4.5 lakh units
sector_themes: Pharma in focus on heavy reporting day: LUPIN (Q4 + recent USFDA nod for ~$337M urea cycle drug), BIOCON (biosimilars), with read-through to SUNPHARMA, DRREDDY, CIPLA; sector defensiveness re-rated as INR firms and crude eases
FMCG/Consumer: DABUR + BRITANNIA results today set rural-vs-urban demand narrative; input-cost relief (palm oil, packaging, fuel) supportive — read-through to HINDUNILVR, NESTLEIND, MARICO, ITC

Auto/auto-ancillary: BHARATFORG (forging/defense), MRF (tyres), ESCORTS (tractors), CRAFTSMAN today — strong April wholesale base from MARUTI/TATAMOTORS/M&M is the tailwind; M&M Q4 PAT +48.5% YoY already reported as bull anchor
Capital goods/Infra: THERMAX result is the order-book bellwether today; reinforces capex theme alongside upcoming ABB (May 8); read-through to SIEMENS, LT, CGPOWER, BEL
Capital markets/exchanges: BSE Q4 today + MCX May 8 — BSE derivatives volume mix and pricing are the key swing factors after recent SEBI/NSE expiry-cycle changes
Energy/Gas: IGL Q4 today — CGD margin tailwind from softer crude/LNG; read-through to MGL, GUJGASLTD, GAIL, ONGC (where lower crude is a negative)
Banking: SBIN Q4 (May 8) is the index-mover with consensus expecting up to -12% PAT on treasury hit; KARURVYSYA today; BANKBARODA May 8; HDFCBANK, ICICIBANK, AXISBANK to react sympathetically
Consumer durables/discretionary: PIDILITIND today, TITAN/ASIANPAINT/KALYANKJIL May 8 — gold/paint commodity prices and rural demand are the variables
risk_flags: Iran ceasefire fragility: MoU is reportedly one-page and unsigned at points; any tanker incident, Strait of Hormuz disruption, or breakdown can re-spike Brent $5-15 intraday and unwind today's risk-on; ~23,000 seafarers still stranded in Persian Gulf
USD/INR at ~94.95 after a 23-paise rebound May 6 from record-low ~95.46 — directional cue tied to crude; any oil reversal pressures rupee back toward 95.50+ and revives FII selling
FII selling persisted (-₹5,835 Cr) even on a +1.24% Nifty day — structural foreign outflow not yet reversed; sustainability of rally depends on FPI flow turn or continued DII absorption
SBIN Q4 (May 8) treasury-loss risk: consensus -12% PAT could pressure PSU bank basket and Bank Nifty; CANBK, PNB, BANKBARODA, UNIONBANK in sympathy zone
Earnings cluster volatility: ~70 prints today + ~76 on May 8 = high single-stock dispersion; misses in heavyweights (DABUR, BRITANNIA, LUPIN, MRF) can cap index upside despite positive cues

Crude reflation tail risk: oil at $100 is still elevated vs trailing average; Exxon CEO commentary flags structural premium; OMC/aviation/paint margin headwinds linger if Brent doesn't sustain sub-$95
Globus/SoftBank-led Nikkei +5% partly reflects Golden Week catch-up — Asian gain may not fully translate; watch for fade if Kospi weakness (-0.68%) signals selective rotation
brief_summary: Indian markets set for a firm gap-up open: GIFT Nifty ~24,521 vs Nifty close 24,330.95 (~+0.7%) on synchronous global risk-on after US-Iran MoU report — S&P/Nasdaq/Russell 2000 at records, Brent down ~9% to ~$100, Nikkei +5%, DAX/FTSE/CAC +2-3%, USD/INR firmer at ~94.95. Trading pivots around a ~70-company earnings deluge today led by DABUR, BRITANNIA, LUPIN, BIOCON, BHARATFORG, MRF, PIDILITIND, THERMAX, BSE, BAJAJHLDNG, IGL — pharma and FMCG most active themes. Bias: positive but watch SBIN Q4 setup tomorrow and any Iran-MoU wobble; FII selling (-₹5,835 Cr May 6) only offset by record DII buying (+₹6,837 Cr) — structural foreign-flow turn not yet confirmed.
